page: Microsoft Azure D8ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1D cache. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression achieving 36.07 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.99 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng registers at 9233.86, while the PassMark CPU score is 16352.95. Memory bandwidth tests show 684.86 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 85.56 GB/sec for large-block reads. For language model workloads, the instance processes 135M model text generation at a strong rate of 266.79 tokens/sec, while 7B model text generation operates at 10.08 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D8ls_v7 offers a high-compute, low-memory profile, making it a resource-dense option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-core ratio and the lack of local storage, requiring external premium storage attachments. This configuration is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, single-threaded data compression, lightweight machine learning inference, and general-purpose computational tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a large 480 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded capabilities, with gzip compression reaching 36.07 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.99 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 266.79 tokens/sec for small language models. Given its low memory-to-core ratio and high clock speed, this instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads, including web serving, data compression, and lightweight inference tasks, though it requires external premium storage for data persistence.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ls_v7 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized virtual machine featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 16.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ls_v7 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized instance with a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 16.0 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A high-clock-speed, low-memory compute instance featuring advanced Intel Xeon processing and top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression
No bundled local storage, but supports Premium Storage attachments
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, web serving, and lightweight model inference
